preamble: Spirantization of the voiced aspirated dental stop [dʱ] into the voiced dental fricative [ð] is a significant shift in historical linguistics, frequently appearing in the development of Greek and Germanic consonant systems.

examples.note: Greek δ (delta) developed from earlier stops to fricatives through spirantization, well-documented in comparative philology.
Verner's Law: PIE aspirated voiced stops [dʱ] became fricatives [ð] in medial position in Proto-Germanic.
